name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 32G Turbo Professional Graphics Card, Reference Design, DDR7 Memory, 3352 TOPS AI Computing Power, DLSS4 Ray Tracing, Ultra-Thin Card Body, Excellent Build Quality
description:
1) Basic Information
Model: GeForce RTX 5090 32G Turbo (Turbo Edition)
GPU Core: GB202 (Blackwell architecture)
Process: TSMC 4nm
CUDA Cores: 21,760
Tensor Cores: 5th Gen, 680
RT Cores: 4th Gen, 170

2) Clocks and Performance
Base Clock: 2,017 MHz
Boost Clock: 2,407 MHz (default) / 2,512 MHz+ (overclocked non-reference model)
Single-Precision Performance: Approx. 78 TFLOPS
AI Performance (INT8): 3,352 TOPS

3) Video Memory
Capacity: 32 GB
Type: GDDR7
Bus width: 512-bit
Memory speed: 28 Gbps
Bandwidth: 1,792 GB/s (1.7 TB/s)

4) Turbo Cooling (Turbo Core Selling Point)
Cooling structure: Dual-slot thickness, single turbo fan, vertical airflow, rear exhaust (heat dissipation outside the chassis)
Fan: 4-pin PWM turbo fan
Dimensions (L × W × H): 269 × 134 × 39 mm (dual-slot)
Weight: Approx. 1,860 g
Bracket Height: 25 mm taller than standard brackets (compatible with server/workstation cases)
PCB / Backplate: All-metal backplate with cooling vents and thermal pads

5) Power Supply and Power Consumption
Typical TDP: 575 W
Peak Power Consumption: 600 W
Power Connector: 16-pin 12VHPWR (ATX 3.1)
Recommended Power Supply: 1,000 W or higher (Gold-rated fully modular)

6) Output Interfaces (Common Configuration for Turbo Edition)
HDMI: 1 × HDMI 2.1b
DisplayPort: 3 × DP 2.1a / 2.1b
Maximum Displays: 4
Maximum Resolution: 7680×4320@120Hz (DSC)

7) Bus and System
Interface: PCIe 5.0 x16
Supported Operating Systems: Windows 11 / Linux (Ubuntu, CentOS, etc.)
API Support: DirectX 12 Ultimate,
